page: OVHcloud h100-760 is a KVM-based GPU-accelerated server featuring 60 dedicated physical cores powered by AMD EPYC 9354 processors running at 3.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 760.0 GB of system memory, yielding 12.67 GB per core. Acceleration is driven by two NVIDIA Hopper H100 GPUs providing a combined 160 GB of VRAM. Local storage consists of 4040 GB of NVMe SSD capacity, and network connectivity is supported by a 16 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates top-tier performance in CPU-intensive and machine learning workloads. The stress-ng single-core and multi-core metrics place the system in the top 10% of tested hardware, supported by a PassMark CPU score of 93,218.13. Large-language model inference benchmarks show top-tier prompt processing speeds, reaching 727.7 tokens per second on a large 70B model and 4,587.57 tokens per second on a medium 7B model. Text generation for the 70B model also registers in the top tier at 29.48 tokens per second.

While the server excels in raw compute, cryptography, and GPU-driven tasks, certain memory and processing subsystems perform within average parameters. Large-block uncached memory read and write bandwidths fall into the middle 50% of benchmarks, at 172.41 GB/sec and 29.16 GB/sec respectively. Similarly, the Geekbench multi-core score of 14,353.0 and static web serving throughput of 24.68 GB/sec represent average tier results. This indicates a resource profile optimized for dense GPU acceleration and dedicated CPU execution rather than general-purpose high-throughput web serving.

Given the dual NVIDIA H100 GPUs and high-capacity NVMe storage, this server is configured for deep learning, LLM training and inference, ray tracing, and complex database operations. The inclusion of dedicated AMD EPYC cores with advanced vector extensions supports high-performance computing simulations and parallelized data processing tasks.
description: OVHcloud h100-760 is a KVM-based GPU-accelerated server designed for intensive computational workloads. It features 60 dedicated AMD EPYC 9354 physical cores operating at 3.0 GHz, paired with 760.0 GB of system memory and 4040 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The primary acceleration is delivered by two NVIDIA Hopper H100 GPUs with a total of 160 GB VRAM. Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance in CPU stress tests and LLM inference, with prompt processing for a 70B model reaching 727.7 tokens per second. However, large-block uncached memory bandwidth and static web serving throughput remain in the average tier. This hardware profile is optimized for deep learning, large-scale LLM inference, ray tracing, and high-performance database operations where dedicated GPU and CPU resources are required.
og_description: OVHcloud h100-760 is a GPU-accelerated server featuring 60 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 760 GB RAM, 4040 GB NVMe storage, and two NVIDIA H100 GPUs with 160 GB total VRAM for top-tier LLM inference.
meta_description: OVHcloud h100-760 is a GPU-accelerated server with 60 AMD EPYC cores, 760 GB RAM, 4040 GB NVMe SSD, and two NVIDIA H100 GPUs for AI and HPC workloads.
tagline: A dual-GPU accelerated server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and NVMe storage for demanding machine learning and compute workloads.
bullet_points: Two NVIDIA Hopper H100 GPUs providing 160 GB of combined VRAM
60 dedicated AMD EPYC 9354 physical cores running at 3.0 GHz
760 GB of system memory and 4040 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier LLM inference prompt processing and text generation performance
16 Gbps baseline network bandwidth and KVM virtualization
Optimized for deep learning, AI model execution, and database operations
